question.subject: cs
question.stem: Convert the 8-bit binary number 01101010 to denary. Show your working.

mark_scheme.description: States the place values for an 8-bit number

student_answer: The place values for an 8-bit number are 128, 64, 32, 16, 8, 4, 2, 1. Reading the bits from the right, the set bits are at 2, 8, 32 and 64, so adding them gives 64+32+8+2=107, which is the denary value.

question.subject: cs
question.stem: State the worst-case time complexity of a linear search over n items and justify your answer.
question.model_answer: O(n) because in the worst case every one of the n items is checked.
question.working: Worst case is the target absent or last; the loop inspects all n elements, so cost grows linearly with n.

mark_scheme.description: Justifies with worst-case scan of every item

student_answer: Linear search runs in O(log n) time, because each comparison effectively halves the remaining items the algorithm still needs to check, similar to how binary search narrows down a sorted list.

question.subject: cs
question.stem: Represent -5 as an 8-bit two's complement binary number. Show your working.

mark_scheme.description: Adds 1 to the inverted bits for the correct final answer

student_answer: 5 in 8-bit binary is 00000101. To get -5 we invert every bit, giving 11111010, and that inverted value is already the two's complement representation of -5, no further step is needed.
